summary: Selected OpenClaw over Hermes Agent as CTO's agent framework

community_evidence.production_deployments: 364K GitHub stars, used by enterprise teams, 25-26 messaging platforms
community_evidence.practitioner_sentiment: Largest open-source agent community. Security concerns acknowledged but mitigable.

evaluation.what_it_makes_obsolete: Custom agent framework. No prior framework existed.

rationale: Evaluated against CTO requirements, not against Hermes features. CTO's core mission is macro evolution (research-driven revolutionary change). OpenClaw's loosely coupled plugin architecture, larger ecosystem (~3,300-5,700 skills), and 25-26 messaging platforms better serve macro evolution than Hermes's tightly coupled self-improvement loop. Hermes excels at micro evolution (40% speedup on repeat tasks via GEPA) but micro evolution is secondary to the mission. Both are MIT licensed. Security concerns exist for both and are mitigable.

test_results.notes: Framework selected based on requirements evaluation, not hands-on testing. Installation research completed.

rollback.instructions: Framework not yet installed. Rollback is simply choosing a different framework.
